decide.condition: Thick mats / dense brush / need to punch through
decide.lure: Sweet Beaver or Rage creature
decide.rig: TX: 4/0-5/0 EWG + 10-14g bullet PEGGED. 50lb braid.

decide.condition: Moderate weeds / weed edges / scattered cover
decide.lure: Rage Craw, Bandito Bug, or Zako
decide.rig: TX: 3/0 EWG + 7g bullet free-slide. 50lb braid + 10-15lb FC leader.

decide.condition: Open water / clean bottom / no snags
decide.lure: Keitech 3.8" or Swimfish on jighead
decide.rig: Jighead 3/0 (exposed hook OK, no weeds). Or SPR-Lock 1/8oz.

decide.lure: Pad Crasher (floats) or Rage Toad (sinks)
decide.rig: Stock frog / 4/0-5/0 EWG. BRAID direct 50-65lb. No leader.

decide.lure: Pad Crasher frog, Rage Toad, Buzzbait. Or: Keitech 2.8" weightless swim above weeds.
decide.rig: SURFACE ONLY. Skip all bottom baits (jig, TX, Ned, yo-yo). They collect algae instantly. Swim baits above the muck or stay on top.

decide.setup: Legend TB + DC (or Zodias for buzzbait)
decide.lure: Irony walk / Pop-X popper / Buzzbait / Poppin Crasher
decide.rig: Stock hooks / VMC snap. BRAID direct. No leader.

decide.lure: Go to Busse Reservoir or Skokie Lagoons (Cook County)
decide.rig: FREE. 20 min drive. Cook County limits. See Spots tab.

decide.lure: Go to Fox River near Algonquin Dam
decide.rig: FREE. 25 min. Dam concentrates fish. Dawn/dusk best for walleye.

decide.lure: Go to Bangs Lake or Diamond Lake
decide.rig: Bangs: 18 min, likely statewide limits. Diamond: 15 min, 24" min 3/day.

decide.lure: Go to Chain O'Lakes State Park
decide.rig: 30 min. Shore limited but Turner Lake accessible. 6,500 acres from boat.

decide.condition: Just want easy fish to eat (catfish, panfish)
decide.lure: Busse Reservoir or any Cook County FP lake
decide.rig: No size limit on catfish or panfish. Keep 6 cat + 15 panfish.

decide.lure: Senko 5" wacky or Ned rig
decide.rig: Wacky: #1 G-Lock + ring. Ned: ShroomZ 1/6oz weedless.

decide.lure: TX creature or spinnerbait 10g or chatterbait
decide.rig: TX: 3/0 EWG + 5-7g bullet. Spinnerbait: stock.

decide.lure: Jig 3/8oz + Rage Craw trailer. Or lipless yo-yo.
decide.rig: Jig: stock 5/0. Lipless (TN70): VMC snap.

decide.lure: DX-Free 3.0 or HU-400 deep crank. Or LV 500 yo-yo.
decide.rig: Stock trebles, VMC snap. Long casts, tip down.

decide.lure: Natural colors: GP creatures, Orbit 110 (silent), Keitech on jighead, Flash-J
decide.rig: Lighter line/leader (8-10lb FC). Subtle presentations.

decide.lure: Chatterbait, Magsquad (rattle), spinnerbait Col/Wil, Rage Craw WM Red
decide.rig: Standard leader 10-15lb FC. Brighter colors.

decide.lure: Spinnerbait Dbl Colorado 23.5g SLOW. Black/Blue creatures. Buzzbait.
decide.rig: Heavy vibration/sound baits. Dark colors. Slow roll.

decide.lure: Lipless burn, spinnerbait fast, Irony topwater, chatterbait
decide.rig: Cover water FAST. Reaction baits. Keep moving.

decide.lure: Swim jig with pauses, TX creature slow drag, Orbit 110 long pause
decide.rig: Slow down. Add pauses. Downsize if needed.

decide.lure: Ned rig. Senko dead-stick. Level Vib finesse. Drop shot.
decide.rig: SLOWEST presentation. Long pauses. Smallest profile. Finesse only.

decide.setup: Legend TB + DC (or Zodias for buzzbait)
decide.lure: Match what theyre eating: Irony (shad), frog (frogs), buzzbait (commotion)
decide.rig: BRAID direct. Speed-match the activity level.

decide.lure: Jerkbaits: Magsquad, Rerange (softest twitches, longest pauses 5-15sec)
decide.rig: VMC snap. 10-12lb FC leader. Patience.

decide.setup: Legend TB + DC or Aspro + DC
decide.lure: Jerkbaits OR lipless ripped through grass. Spinnerbait slow roll.
decide.rig: Multiple options. Start jerkbait, switch if no response 30min.

decide.lure: TX creatures, chatterbait, swim jig, topwater dawn, Senko
decide.rig: Most versatile window. Everything works. Cover water.

decide.setup: Legend TB (dawn topwater) then Expride (shade finesse)
decide.lure: Dawn: Irony/buzzbait 90min. After: Ned/Senko in shade ONLY.
decide.rig: Dont fish exposed banks midday. Shade or deep only.

decide.lure: Finesse: Senko, Ned, Fluke weightless. Or: topwater walk (Irony).
decide.rig: Calm = fish spook easy. Lighter presentations. Longer casts.

decide.lure: Spinnerbait 10g, chatterbait, lipless. Wind-blown bank = baitfish = bass.
decide.rig: Fish the wind-blown bank (bait concentrates there). Reaction baits.

decide.lure: Heavy spinnerbaits (17-24g), Texas rig (heavy enough to cast in wind)
decide.rig: Need weight to cast into wind. Stay on windblown banks.

decide.lure: Large spinnerbait slow roll. Swim jig. Jockie 120.
decide.rig: WIRE LEADER MANDATORY. Big profile 120mm+ minimum.

decide.lure: Katana 130SP jerkbait. Krocodile 24g. Large chatterbait.
decide.rig: WIRE. Cast parallel to weed edge. Pause on transitions.

decide.lure: Same lure, add 3-5sec to pauses
decide.rig: Dont change lure first. Change cadence.

decide.lure: Ned rig GP (weedless 1/6oz)
decide.rig: Cast, sink, tiny hops, long pauses. Dead simple.

decide.lure: Senko 5" wacky (GP or Blk/Blue per clarity)
decide.rig: Cast, let fall, DO NOT RETRIEVE. Watch line.

decide.lure: Same bait that was working earlier today (or Ned/Senko)
decide.rig: If 10 casts with no bite = fish arent there. MOVE.

now.if_not_working: Expride Spinning: RAID Level Vib finesse yo-yo. Ned rig ultra-slow.
Zodias MH: Jackall TN70 ripped through grass. Spinnerbait 10g slow roll.
now.where: Points to spawning flats, windblown banks

now.throw_first: Senko 5" wacky: let fall, dont retrieve
now.if_not_working: Aspro + DC: Texas rig creature weed edges. Zodias MH: spinnerbait 10g over weed tops (if thick cover nearby, switch to Zodias for power).
now.where: Protected pockets, shallow hard bottom, back of creeks

now.throw_first: ZipBaits Irony walk or Pop-X pop dawn. Chatterbait midday.
now.if_not_working: Expride Spinning: floating worm surface. Zodias MH: swim jig weed edges.
now.where: First breakline outside spawn areas, shaded docks

now.throw_first: ZipBaits Irony walk or Pop-X pop or Whopper Plopper dawn 90min. Jig or deep crank midday.
now.if_not_working: Expride Spinning: Ned rig in shade. Senko dead-sticked.
now.where: Dawn: shallow pts. After 9am: shade, deep rock, bridges

now.throw_first: Dawn: Irony or Buzzbait or Whopper Plopper. Day: HU-400 deep or shade jig.
now.if_not_working: Expride Spinning: Senko in deepest shade. Find current (inflows).
now.where: Current areas, bridge shade, offshore humps. Dawn/dusk ONLY.

now.season: Fall 65-55F BEST bank fishing (Sep - mid Oct)
now.setup: Aspro + DC (cover water fast: lipless, spinnerbait, chatterbait)
now.if_not_working: Aspro + DC: swim jig weed edges. Legend TB + DC: Orbit 110 on windy open points.
now.where: Creek arms, shallow pts near deep, windblown banks w/ bait

now.if_not_working: Zodias MH: jig slow drag deepest point. Expride: Ned rig dead-stick.
now.where: Deep points, bluff walls, riprap with deep water access

now.season: Salmon Run 45-55F (Sep - Oct, Lake Michigan tribs)
now.throw_first: Krocodile 24g: cast upstream, let swing in current
now.if_not_working: Aspro + DC: K.O. Wobbler lighter current. Expride: Vibrax 10g pools.
now.where: River mouths, current seams, tailouts, deep pools

now.throw_first: Spinnerbait slow roll shallow + WIRE LEADER
now.setup: Zodias MH + Revo SX + titanium wire
now.if_not_working: Legend TB + wire: Katana 130SP jerkbait in 2-5ft.
now.where: Shallow weedy bays, creek inflows, dark bottom areas

now.throw_first: Swim jig or Katana 130SP along weed edges + WIRE
now.setup: Zodias MH + Revo SX + titanium wire
now.where: Weed edges both sides, rock-to-weed transitions

now.season: Suburban Lake - moderate weeds (Green Lake type)
now.throw_first: Ned rig weedless GP. Senko 5" wacky. Jerkbaits along edges.
now.setup: Expride Spinning (finesse) + Legend TB or Aspro (jerkbaits/search)
now.if_not_working: Aspro + DC: Keitech 3.8" SPR-Lock along weed edges. Spinnerbait 10g over tops.
now.where: Weed edges, open pockets, deeper areas, docks. Has fishable bottom.

now.season: Suburban Pond - heavy algae/weeds (Old Farm type)
now.throw_first: Pad Crasher frog walked over surface. Rage Toad buzzed. Buzzbait.
now.setup: Zodias MH + Revo SX, BRAID direct (surface baits need power + braid)
now.if_not_working: Expride: Keitech 2.8" WEIGHTLESS (no bullet, swim above weeds). White Fluke twitched near surface.
now.where: Anywhere with surface mats or algae. Skip bottom-contact baits entirely (bottom is unfishable).

spots.limits: 6 bass (14"+), 6 catfish (any size), 15 panfish (any size), 2 poles max
spots.access: EXCELLENT. 6 fishing walls, paved paths, bait sold on-site. 457 acres.
spots.fees: FREE parking, FREE launch (electric only, no gas)
spots.notes: YOUR #1 SPOT. Biggest water nearby with real infrastructure. Cook County limits = 6x more bass than Lake County. Sunrise-sunset. Address: Higgins Rd & I-290 (Elk Grove Village). Boating Center: 847-640-0027.

spots.limits: 6 bass (14"+), 6 catfish, 15 panfish. Same Cook County limits.
spots.access: Good. 4 entrances, accessible fishing at Tower Rd. 242 acres, 7 lagoons, 15ft deep.
spots.notes: Multiple connected lagoons to explore. If walleye/pike reports true = great variety. Tower Rd has boat launch (kayak/canoe). Tower Rd entrance: Tower Rd east of Edens Expy (I-94), Winnetka.

spots.limits: Same Cook County limits. 6 bass, 6 cat, 15 pan.
spots.access: Good. 37 acres, 18.5ft deep, island structure. Connects to Des Plaines River Trail.
spots.notes: Good depth + island = structure for bigger fish. Can also fish Des Plaines River same trip.

spots.access: Available but limited detail. Shore only (no boat launch).
spots.notes: CLOSEST Cook County option. Quick evening trip.

spots.species: Smallmouth, walleye, flathead catfish, white bass, panfish
spots.limits: STATEWIDE: 6 bass, 6 walleye (14"+), no limit catfish, 15 panfish. Best regs anywhere.
spots.access: Dam area concentrates fish. Municipal park access. Limited boat ramps = less boat traffic.
spots.notes: Nearest WALLEYE + SMALLMOUTH fishery. Real river, not a pond. Flatheads can be huge. Fish dam tailwater. Algonquin Dam: IL-62 (Algonquin Rd) bridge area, Algonquin IL.

spots.limits: Statewide or Cook County limits depending on section.
spots.access: Des Plaines River Trail provides extensive bank access. Beck Lake connects.
spots.notes: River variety close to home. Can combine with Beck Lake trip.

spots.species: LMB, walleye, pike, MUSKIE, catfish, crappie, white bass
spots.limits: STATEWIDE: 6 bass, 6 walleye (14"+), 3 pike (24"+), 1 muskie (36"+), no limit catfish
spots.access: LIMITED from shore (boat fishery). Turner Lake in park has bank spots. Kayak rental available.
spots.notes: PREMIER multi-species. Everything in one place. Consider kayak for full access. 6,500 acres. Address: 8916 Wilmot Rd, Spring Grove IL. Call: 847-587-5512.

spots.species: LMB, northern PIKE, catfish, bluegill, perch
spots.limits: Likely statewide (not LCFPD). Call to confirm: 847-526-3610.
spots.access: Public park areas. Natural glacial lake, 32ft deep.
spots.fees: Shore: FREE. Boat launch: $25. Kayak rental: $20/2hr.
spots.notes: Closest lake with PIKE. Real depth (32ft). Natural lake not a retention pond.

spots.limits: STATEWIDE: 6 bass (no size min), 6 walleye (14"+), no limit panfish. Confirm w/ park district.
spots.access: Good bank access. 5.5 acres, likely 12-15ft deep. Stocked Nov 2024.
spots.notes: Has WALLEYE (dawn/dusk). Stocked. 1101 N Green Knolls Dr.

spots.limits: Likely statewide. Confirm w/ park district: 847-850-2100.
spots.access: Bank access listed as amenity. Small (~3-5 acres), shallow.

spots.limits: Site-specific: 3 bass (15"+), 3 pike (24"+), 1 muskie (48"!), 6 catfish. 2 poles max.
spots.access: Bob Lewandowski Park south side. Limited to one access area.
spots.notes: Trophy MUSKIE managed (48" min). Also pike. More restrictive than Cook County.

spots.species: LMB, pike, muskie, walleye, crappie, bluegill
spots.limits: CATCH AND RELEASE ONLY. Cannot keep any fish.
spots.access: Excellent. 110 acres, 50ft deep, 4.3 miles of shoreline.
spots.fees: FREE (Lake County residents). Parking fee for non-residents.
spots.notes: Best shore access + biggest lake nearby. But C&R only. Good for practice/fun, not keeping.

setups.name: Heavy Duty / Pike
setups.line: 50lb braid DIRECT (no leader, never). Permanent config.
setups.grab_for: Frogs (all 3), Rage Toad, buzzbait, swim jigs, spinnerbaits 17g+, flipping jigs + Texas rig in THICK cover only, heavy lipless grass ripping
setups.note: DEFAULT LURE: Pitching jig 3/8oz + Rage Craw. BRAID DIRECT always. No leader = no retying. Only for surface baits + heavy cover where stealth doesnt matter.

setups.name: Primary / Versatile
setups.line: 50lb braid + 10-15lb fluoro LEADER (always on). Re-spool to 30lb braid when worn.
setups.grab_for: YOUR 70% ROD: TX rig creatures (weed edges), jigs (moderate cover), chatterbait, spinnerbait 10g, spoons, medium cranks, lipless yo-yo, swimbaits 3.8-4.8"
setups.note: DEFAULT LURE: Spinnerbait 10g Col/Wil. Leader ALWAYS ON (stealth for underwater presentations). Never remove leader — just cut and retie to lure.

setups.name: Jerkbait / Topwater
setups.line: 10lb Seaguar InvizX FLUORO DIRECT (no braid, no leader). Permanent config.
setups.grab_for: ALL jerkbaits 110-130mm, walk-the-dog (Irony), Pop-X popper, New Vision subsurface walk
setups.note: DEFAULT LURE: Orbit 110. FLUORO DIRECT always. Best suspend action + invisible. Topwater still works on 10lb fluoro.

setups.name: Finesse / Spinning
setups.line: 30lb braid + 10lb fluoro LEADER (always on). Re-spool to 10-15lb braid when worn.
setups.grab_for: Senko wacky, Super Fluke weightless (4/0 EWG), Ned rig, drop shot (Tiny Fluke), Rozante 77SP, Keitech 2.8", Flash-J, spinners, grubs, tubes
setups.note: DEFAULT LURE: Senko 5" wacky GP. Leader ALWAYS ON. Super Fluke now lives here (weightless twitch on spinning = better cast + same slack-line technique as Senko).

setups.name: Pike / Salmon
setups.line: Any casting setup + titanium wire leader 25-30cm (replaces fluoro leader)
setups.grab_for: Spoons, large spinnerbaits, large jerkbaits targeting pike. WIRE MANDATORY.
setups.note: Swap fluoro leader for titanium wire on pike days. Wire ties to braid same as fluoro (FG or Alberto knot to a swivel, or direct crimp).

howto.rod_position: SNAP = sharp 6-10in wrist movement, rod tip down (makes bait dart sideways). TWITCH = gentler 2-4in movement (finesse). WALK-THE-DOG = rhythmic slack-snap-slack making bait glide L-R on surface. POP = sharp snap with cupped-face bait creating splash/bloop sound. DRAG = pull bait with rod (not reel) along bottom. HOP = pop rod tip 4-6in to lift bait off bottom.

BRAID DIRECT = straight braid to lure, no leader (frogs, toad, buzzbait). FLUORO LEADER = braid mainline + fluorocarbon leader tied with knot (everything else unless noted). TX RIG = Texas rig: bullet weight + EWG offset hook through soft plastic (weedless). WACKY = hook through middle of worm (not nose). DROP SHOT = weight below, hook above on leader.

JIGHEAD = exposed hook molded into lead head. Best hookup ratio but SNAGS in cover. Use in OPEN WATER. TX RIG (EWG) = bullet weight + hook point buried in plastic. WEEDLESS but slightly fewer hookups. Use in WEEDS/COVER. Your lakes are weedy = TX rig or weighted weedless hook is often safer choice.

Aspro ALWAYS implies Curado DC paired with it. 'Aspro + DC' = Graphiteleader Aspro rod + Shimano Curado DC reel. If a lure says 'Zodias MH + Revo or Aspro + DC' it means either power setup OR versatile setup works for that bait.
42-55F = deep/points (jerkbaits). 55-65F = spawn shallow (senko). 65-80F = shallow dawn, deep mid. 80F+ = dawn/dusk only.
howto.motion: Same spot/depth all day. Not moving after 10 biteless casts. Staying shallow after 9am summer.
howto.line_mgmt: Wind-blown banks = bait concentrates = bass follow. Dark bottom warms first. Find baitfish = find bass.

howto.technique: GLOSSARY: Just Reel vs Rod Moves
howto.rod_position:
JUST REEL (rod stays still, reel does all work): Crankbaits, spinnerbaits, swim jigs, buzzbaits, Rage Toad, inline spinners, spoons, swimbaits (Keitech/Swimfish steady), chatterbaits. Rule: if it has a blade, a bill, or a paddle tail = just reel.

ROD MOVES THE BAIT (reel only manages slack): Jerkbaits (snap down), walk-the-dog (twitch sideways), poppers (snap down into cup), Texas rig/jigs (hop up, drag sideways, shake), lipless yo-yo (sweep up, let fall), fluke weightless (twitch down on slack), Ned rig (tiny hops). Rule: if it's a soft plastic or a suspending/sinking hard bait without a bill = rod moves it.
DOWN 45deg toward water (8 oclock). Tip 6-12in above surface.
howto.motion: WRIST snap down (6-10in tip travel). Elbow stays still. Crisp flick, not power pull.
howto.line_mgmt: Slight BOW in line during pause (not tight, not slack). Reel 1-2 cranks ONLY to gather slack after snap. Reel 2-3 cranks after long pause to recover distance. Reel NEVER moves the bait.
howto.pause: 3-10sec (cold=longer). The pause catches fish. If followers wont commit: add 3 more seconds.
howto.hookset: Reel down until tight, then sweep SIDEWAYS (not up). Side sweep drives trebles into jaw corner.
howto.key_rule: The bait darts on the SNAP. It catches fish on the PAUSE. Reel is only slack management.

howto.rod_position: 10 oclock (slightly above horizontal), pointed at bait. This is your HOME position between movements.
howto.motion:
THREE techniques with different rod motions:
HOP (vertical): Lift tip UP from 10 to 12 oclock (4-6in). Jig hops off bottom. Drop back to 10. Let fall.
DRAG (horizontal): Sweep rod SIDEWAYS from 9 to 11 oclock, pulling jig ALONG bottom (crawling, not lifting). Reel up slack. Repeat.
SHAKE (in place): Tiny 1-2in vibrations at rod tip. Skirt/trailer pulse without jig moving forward.
howto.line_mgmt: Semi-slack on the fall (feel bottom but dont pull bait sideways). Watch line for tick/jump/sideways movement on fall. 70% of bites on the FALL.
howto.pause: 2-3sec after each hop. Let skirt/trailer settle. Let it sit on bottom.
howto.hookset: Reel down to fish weight, then SWEEP HARD to side. MH power drives hook through plastic + jaw.
howto.key_rule: HOP = lifts jig off bottom (vertical). DRAG = crawls jig along bottom (horizontal). Different triggers. Hop for reaction, drag for following the bottom contour and feeling structure. Alternate both.

howto.rod_position: Point at bait after cast. No specific angle needed — just watch line.
howto.motion: NOTHING. Do not move the bait. Cast, let fall. Thats it. One tiny twitch after bottom, then let fall again.
howto.line_mgmt: Semi-slack. Enough to see line movement but not pulling the bait. The shimmy on fall IS the action.
howto.pause: The entire technique is a pause. Fall = 100% of the action. If it hits bottom: one twitch, let fall again, then reel in.
howto.hookset: Watch line: jumps, moves sideways, goes slack, or feels heavy. Sweep set to side.
howto.key_rule: DO NOT RETRIEVE. Cast, let fall, watch line. 70% of bites on initial fall. One cast = one spot.

howto.rod_position: 9 oclock (HORIZONTAL, parallel to water). Pointed toward bait, slightly off to your dominant side. Tip near water surface.
howto.motion: Same sideways wrist twitch EVERY TIME (always to the same side). You do NOT alternate left-right. The slack between twitches lets the baits momentum carry it the opposite direction automatically. Same flick, same direction, over and over. The bait zigzags on its own from the physics of slack + weighted tail. 2-3 inch tip movement. Quick and light, not powerful.
howto.line_mgmt: Slack is what makes it walk. After each twitch: return rod tip to create slack (bait momentum carries it gliding). Too tight = bait dives or wont glide. No reeling between twitches usually — slack forms naturally when you return rod tip.
howto.pause: Optional: after 4-5 walks, dead stop 2-3sec. Tail settles, final sound. Then restart. Triggers followers.
howto.hookset: WAIT 1-2 SECONDS after blowup. They often miss first strike. Set on WEIGHT not splash. Sweep side.
howto.key_rule: You twitch ONE direction only. Bait walks L-R by itself (slack creates the zigzag). If bait only goes one way and wont walk: not enough slack between twitches. If bait dives or skips: twitching too hard. Think tapping, not snapping.

howto.rod_position: 8 oclock (angled DOWN toward water, tip close to surface). Lower than walk-the-dog because you need downward force to make the cup spit.
howto.motion: Short sharp DOWNWARD wrist snap (different from walk-the-dog which is sideways). The downward pop drives the cupped face into the water surface = spit/splash. 2-4 inch downward tip movement.
howto.line_mgmt: Small slack between pops (similar to walk-the-dog). Reel 1 crank between pops to recover.
howto.pause: 2-3sec between pops. Pop-pop-pause is the basic cadence. Also: single pop then 5sec dead sit.
howto.hookset: Same as walk-the-dog: WAIT 1-2sec after blowup. Set on weight.
howto.key_rule: Different trigger than walk-the-dog. Pop = spit/splash sound. Use when walking gets refused. Calm mornings, docks.

howto.rod_position: FROG/TOAD: rod tip medium-high. BUZZBAIT: rod tip HIGH (10-11 oclock).
howto.motion: FROG: walk-the-dog over mats OR slow crawl. TOAD: fast steady reel (legs churn). BUZZBAIT: fast steady reel (prop churns). Toad and buzz SINK if you stop!
howto.line_mgmt: BRAID DIRECT (no leader). Braid floats (helps surface) + zero stretch (instant hookset through soft body/cover).
howto.pause: FROG: pause at mat holes and edges. TOAD/BUZZ: do NOT pause (they sink). Stop only in open pockets briefly.
howto.hookset: WAIT 1-2 SECONDS. Universal rule for all surface baits. Set on WEIGHT. Sweep hard — need force to drive hook through soft plastic body AND pull fish from cover.
howto.key_rule: Bend frog hook points out 1-2mm. Trim frog legs if short strikes. Braid is MANDATORY (no fluoro ever).

howto.rod_position: 9-10 oclock (horizontal or slightly down). Lower tip = bait runs deeper. Higher = shallower.
howto.motion: SLOW ROLL: Steady reel, barely feel blade vibrate. Thats the correct speed. BURN: fast reel near surface (summer). HELICOPTER: let fall on slack off ledges (blades spin on descent).
howto.line_mgmt: Keep line reasonably taut (need to feel blade vibration through the rod). If you cant feel thumping, speed up slightly.
howto.pause: Not a pause-bait typically. But: killing it briefly near cover can trigger followers. Also: helicopter fall IS a pause technique (controlled fall).
howto.hookset: The fish hooks itself on the single hook. Just keep reeling into the weight. No hard snap needed — sweep into it.
howto.key_rule: If strong vibration = too fast for cold water. Barely-perceptible thump = correct cold water speed. Speed up as water warms.

howto.rod_position: 9-10 oclock. Raise tip = bait rides higher. Lower = deeper. Keep above weed tops.
howto.motion: STEADY retrieve ~1 handle turn per second. NOT hopping (thats a bottom jig technique). Speed through thick cover, slow on open stretches.
howto.line_mgmt: Keep taut enough to feel the jig tracking and any strikes. Braid OK (floats, keeps jig higher in column).
howto.pause: Mix in 2-3sec pauses to trigger followers. Rage Craw trailer FLUTTERS on the pause (thats the trigger). Space Monkey keeps thumping on pause (different trigger).
howto.hookset: Feel weight or a thud = sweep set to side. Often hits on the pause/fall.
howto.key_rule: Steady swim, not hopping. Think weedless spinnerbait. If blade version fouls on weeds: switch to bladeless Rage Craw version.

howto.rod_position: Start at 8 oclock (low). Sweep UP to 11 oclock for the lift.
howto.motion: SMOOTH sweep up (not violent rip unless grass). Let fall on SEMI-SLACK. Also: steady medium retrieve, or high-speed burn-and-kill (reel fast then dead stop).
howto.line_mgmt: SEMI-SLACK on fall is critical. Not tight (kills flutter), not fully slack (cant detect bite). Slight bow that straightens = bite. RIPPING GRASS: feel bait load up, sharp upward snap to pop free, then let flutter fall.
howto.pause: The fall IS the technique. Each lift-fall cycle is the trigger. 1-2 seconds between yo-yos. Bite comes within first second of fall.
howto.hookset: IMMEDIATELY on anything weird during the fall. Line tick, jump, slack, sideways movement = set hook NOW. Reel down, sweep side.
howto.key_rule: Most bites on the FALL (like Texas rig). Semi-slack lets you detect them. Tight line = missed bites.

howto.rod_position: 9-10 oclock (low). Rod absorbs deflections. Let the rod load, dont fight it.
howto.motion: STEADY RETRIEVE. Not stop-and-go (usually). Reel at medium speed to maintain depth and wobble. Throw AT cover — deflection off rocks/stumps/wood = strike trigger.
howto.line_mgmt: Relatively taut. You need to feel the bait's wobble and any contact with structure. Fluoro direct (no braid) is ideal — stretch keeps trebles pinned on headshakes.
howto.pause: Generally not a pause bait. Exception: Flap Slap (rod drag then pause, nose kicks up). Exception: MD-X Cyclone (stop for spiral drift). Exception: HU-400 (stop for riser action).
howto.hookset: DO NOT SET HOOK. Just keep reeling. Trebles hook the fish on their own during the deflection bite. Hard hooksets RIP trebles out.
howto.key_rule: Max cast = max depth. Throw AT objects (deflection triggers strikes). If it feels like you snagged then came free = FISH (keep reeling).

howto.rod_position: 10 oclock or wherever comfortable. Nothing specific required.
howto.motion: Cast, let sink. TINY hops: 2-3 inches of rod tip movement. Long drags along bottom. Shake in place. Less is more.
howto.line_mgmt: Light tension. Feel bottom contact. These bites are often subtle (just feels slightly heavier).
howto.pause: LONG pauses between every movement (3-5sec minimum). The tail standing up on bottom IS working even when you do nothing. Let it sit.
howto.hookset: Firm sweep (not violent snap). Light wire hooks on ShroomZ can bend if you snap too hard.
howto.key_rule: Dont overthink it. Cast, sink, tiny movements, long pauses. The simplest technique in fishing. Less = more.

howto.rod_position: LOW at 8-9 oclock (down toward water), pointed toward bait. Slack line critical.
howto.motion: Twitch-twitch-PAUSE. Sharp 6-10in wrist snaps on SLACK LINE. The slack = lateral dart. Tight line = bait swims toward you (wrong).
howto.line_mgmt: SLACK LINE IS MANDATORY. Each snap whips bait through the slack. After snaps: return rod to position, reel 1-2 cranks to reset. During pause: let line bow slightly.
howto.pause: 2-5sec (where bites happen). Bait sinks slowly, shimmying. Watch line for any movement. Extend to 8-10sec in cold.
howto.hookset: Reel down to weight, then SWEEP side. Do not snap-set. The offset hook needs sustained pressure to rotate and penetrate.
howto.key_rule: If bait swims toward you instead of darting sideways = your line is too tight. Create slack before snapping.

howto.rod_position: Wherever comfortable. 9-10 oclock works.
howto.motion: STEADY retrieve. Count down for depth control (count 3 = shallow, count 7 = deeper). Adjust speed to feel blade spin.
howto.line_mgmt: ALWAYS use ball bearing swivel between braid and leader/spinner (prevents line twist that destroys your line). Keep tension to feel blade rotation.
howto.pause: Not typically a pause bait. Steady does the work. But: killing it near a target lets it flutter-fall briefly = different trigger.
howto.hookset: Keep reeling into the fish. Single hook finds its own purchase. Light sweep if needed.
howto.key_rule: SWIVEL MANDATORY. Without it, the spinning blade twists your line into an unusable mess within 20 casts.

howto.rod_position: 9-10 oclock. Adjust height to control depth.
howto.motion: SLOW steady retrieve. Feel tail thumping through rod. If bait rises in water column = too fast. If you cant feel thump = too slow.
howto.line_mgmt: Moderate tension. Need to feel the tail kick rhythm. Jighead (open water) vs weighted EWG hook (cover/weeds).
howto.pause: Optional 1-2sec pauses (bait flutters on fall = trigger). Keitecth especially kicks tail on the fall too.
howto.hookset: Sweep set. If on jighead (exposed hook) = fish often hooks itself. If on TX/weighted hook = need a more deliberate set.
howto.key_rule: SLOW. Slower than you think. Match actual baitfish speed. Keitech tears easily — use TwistLOCK hooks and super-glue head.

lures.name: ZipBaits Orbit 110 SP (Acid Green)
lures.hook_weight: Stock #6 trebles. Owner Hyper Snap (or split ring + VMC snap).
lures.retrieve: ROD MOVES: Snap-snap-pause 3-10sec. Rod tip DOWN 45deg (8 oclock). Wrist only.
lures.tip: SILENT. Acid Green = high-vis reaction color (stained water, low light). Cold water = 10-15sec pauses. Dont reel between snaps. For natural/clear conditions, use Ito-Shiner TN Shad instead.

lures.name: Jackall Magsquad 115 SP
lures.hook_weight: Stock #5 trebles. Owner Hyper Snap (or split ring + VMC snap).
lures.retrieve: ROD MOVES: Hard snap-snap-pause 3-8sec. Or: 1 hard rip then 5-10sec dead stop.
lures.tip: RATTLE = stained water. Instant-stop feature. Only BRIGHT GREEN color remains (natural color lost). Consider replacing natural color.

lures.name: Jackall Rerange 110 SP
lures.hook_weight: Stock #6 trebles. Owner Hyper Snap (or split ring + VMC snap).
lures.retrieve: ROD MOVES: SOFT twitch-pause 8-15sec (flat sides amplify). Gentle input!
lures.tip: COLD WATER specialist. Flat = intense flash. SOFTER snaps than other jerkbaits.

lures.name: Duo Rozante 77SP
lures.retrieve: ROD MOVES: Micro-twitches, 2-5sec pause. Very gentle. Or slow steady roll.
lures.setup: Expride Spinning (too light for casting gear)
lures.tip: FINESSE jerkbait. When big jerkbaits get refused. Clear + pressured.

lures.name: Megabass Ito-Shiner (TN Shad + Stain Reaction OB)
lures.retrieve: ROD MOVES: Aggressive snap-snap-snap-pause 2-5sec. Needs HARD input. Darts 30% farther than Vision 110.
lures.tip: 2 colors ordered: TN Shad (natural/clear) + Stain Reaction OB (murky). Unique erratic dart nothing else replicates.

lures.name: Gan Craft Rest 108
lures.retrieve: ROD MOVES: Snap-snap then STOP. Let RISE 3-8sec. The RISE is the trigger.
lures.tip: LOST. Slow-floating minnow. Rising action on pause was unique trigger. Consider replacing if you miss the float-up presentation.

lures.name: Jackall Mickey Slim 128
lures.retrieve: ROD MOVES: Slow STEADY retrieve creating wake. NOT a jerkbait - dont snap hard.
lures.tip: Steady swim bait. 128mm pike target. Stop-and-go triggers followers.

lures.name: Megabass New Vision 100
lures.retrieve: ROD MOVES: Subsurface walk-the-dog: rhythmic L-R twitches JUST under surface.
lures.tip: When fish refuse topwater. Works 10-20cm below surface film.

lures.name: ZipBaits Irony 90
lures.retrieve: ROD MOVES: Walk-the-dog: rod HORIZONTAL (9 oclock), small SIDEWAYS wrist twitches (2-3in), slack between each. Rhythm: twitch-slack-twitch-slack.
lures.setup: Legend TB + DC #2. 10lb fluoro OK for topwater walk.
lures.tip: LOUDEST walker. WAIT 1-2sec after blowup before hookset.

lures.name: Jackall Jockie 120
lures.retrieve: ROD MOVES: Random cadence: horizontal twitches (walk), downward snaps (pop/dive), pauses. Chaos not rhythm.
lures.tip: DISCONTINUED. Vary inputs constantly. Vertical hang on pause = strike window.

lures.name: Megabass Pop-X (GG Wild Gill)
lures.hook_weight: Stock #6 trebles. Owner Hyper Snap or split ring + VMC snap.
lures.retrieve: ROD MOVES: Short sharp POPS: wrist snap down, pause 2-3sec between pops. Pop-pop-pause. Also: pop then let sit 5sec.
lures.tip: YOUR ONLY POPPER. Different trigger than walk-the-dog. Imitates bluegill struggling on surface. GG Wild Gill = bluegill match for suburban lakes. WAIT on hookset.

lures.name: Booyah Pad Crasher
lures.retrieve: JUST REEL: Walk-the-dog: rod HORIZONTAL, small sideways twitches with slack between. Slow crawl over thick mats.
lures.setup: Zodias MH + Revo SX. BRAID direct 50-65lb.
lures.tip: WAIT 1-2sec after blowup! Bend hooks out 1-2mm. Trim legs.

lures.name: Booyah Pad Crasher Jr
lures.retrieve: JUST REEL: Same horizontal walk as standard. Use when standard gets refused.
lures.setup: Zodias MH + Revo SX. BRAID direct.
lures.tip: When standard gets refused. Extra patience on hookset.

lures.name: Booyah Poppin Pad Crasher
lures.retrieve: JUST REEL: POP: rod angled DOWN (8 oclock), short downward wrist snap (cup spits water). WALK: rod horizontal, sideways twitches. Combine both.
lures.setup: Zodias MH + Revo SX. BRAID direct.
lures.tip: 3 techniques in one: pop, walk, or combo. Pop cuts through wind/slop.

lures.name: Strike King Rage Toad 4"
lures.retrieve: JUST REEL: Fast steady reel, rod HIGH. Sinks if you stop! Start IMMEDIATELY.
lures.setup: Zodias MH + Revo SX. BRAID direct 40-65lb.
lures.tip: Louder than frog but SINKS. Must reel fast. WAIT on hookset.

lures.name: Buzzbait White/Chartreuse
lures.retrieve: JUST REEL: Fast steady, rod HIGH. Prop churns surface. NEVER stop reeling.
lures.setup: Zodias MH + Revo SX. BRAID direct 40-50lb.
lures.tip: LOUDEST surface bait. Dawn/dusk/rain. WAIT on hookset like frog.

lures.name: River2Sea Whopper Plopper 90
lures.hook_weight: Stock trebles. VMC snap or Owner Hyper Snap.
lures.retrieve: JUST REEL: Steady retrieve (prop sputters). Vary speed: fast=loud, slow=subtle. STOP-AND-GO: reel then pause (it FLOATS, unlike buzzbait). Fish hit on the pause.
lures.setup: Legend TB + DC #2 (10lb fluoro) or Aspro + DC. Open water — trebles snag in thick cover.
lures.tip: FLOATS on pause (unique vs buzzbait which sinks). Stop-and-go is the key technique. NOT weedless (treble hooks). Summer 65F+, dawn/dusk, open water or sparse cover. WAIT on hookset.

lures.name: 6th Sense Crush 100X
lures.retrieve: JUST REEL: Medium steady INTO cover. Keep reeling through contact.
lures.tip: Throw AT objects. Deflection = trigger. Dont set hook, just reel.

lures.name: Lucky Craft LC 1.5
lures.retrieve: JUST REEL: Moderate bumping. Pause 1sec (floats off snags). Slow roll pressured.
lures.setup: Aspro + DC (12g lighter) or Zodias MH
lures.tip: SILENT + high-float = pressured fish killer. Replace hooks immediately.

lures.name: Megabass Flap Slap
lures.retrieve: JUST REEL: ROD drag (not reel) forward, pause 1-2sec (nose kicks UP). NOT steady crank!
lures.tip: COLD WATER hybrid crank/jerkbait. Rod drags + pauses. Not straight retrieve.

lures.name: Usami Purin XL 55 F-DR
lures.retrieve: JUST REEL: Steady retrieve grinding bottom 5-8ft. Stop-and-go.
lures.tip: Compact body reaches medium depth. Replace stock hooks.

lures.name: Megabass DX-Free 3.0
lures.retrieve: JUST REEL: Steady retrieve tip DOWN (submerge tip +2-3ft). LONG CASTS.
lures.tip: Max cast = max depth. Need 60ft+ casts. 10-12lb fluoro only.

lures.name: Megabass MD-X Cyclone
lures.retrieve: JUST REEL: 4-5 cranks then STOP 2-3sec (bait SPIRALS upward). Repeat.
lures.tip: Unique SPIRAL rise on pause. Stop-and-go is mandatory.

lures.name: Hideup HU-400
lures.retrieve: JUST REEL: Crank deep, STOP, let RISE with drunken wobble 3-5sec. Repeat.
lures.tip: RISER ACTION on stop = trigger. Need 60-80ft casts. Summer deep only.

lures.name: Jackall TN70
lures.retrieve: JUST REEL: Yo-yo: smooth lift 8-11 oclock, fall semi-slack. Rip through grass.
lures.tip: TIGHT vibration (not thump). Moderate speed. Bites on fall = line tick.

lures.name: Lucky Craft LV 500
lures.retrieve: JUST REEL: Power yo-yo deep (10-20ft). Burn max speed. Heavy = fast fall.
lures.tip: HEAVIEST/LOUDEST. Chartreuse Shad = stained-muddy water. Distance king.

lures.name: Cordell Super Spot
lures.retrieve: JUST REEL: Slow roll. RISES on pause (pops off snags). Grass-top burn.
lures.tip: BUOYANT = rises on pause (unique). Royal Red = crawfish. Upgrade hooks.

lures.name: RAID JAPAN Level Vib
lures.retrieve: ROD MOVES: Finesse yo-yo SPINNING GEAR. Gentle lift, slow flutter-fall.
lures.tip: SPINNING ONLY. Smallest/quietest. When big lipless refused. Light tackle.

lures.name: Booyah One Knocker
lures.retrieve: JUST REEL: SLOW yo-yo emphasizing thump cadence. Crawl-and-thump in cold.
lures.tip: LOST. Single THUMP not rattle. Different sound catches conditioned fish.

lures.name: Krocodile 24g
lures.retrieve: JUST REEL: Cast upstream/across, let current swing. Barely retrieve.
lures.tip: Let current do 70% of work. Dont retrieve against current.

lures.name: K.O. Wobbler 14g
lures.retrieve: JUST REEL: Slow retrieve with wobble. Count down for depth (1sec=~1ft).
lures.tip: More wobble than Krocodile. Lighter = slower/shallower water.

lures.name: Moonshine Super Glow 14.7g
lures.retrieve: JUST REEL: Slow retrieve. CHARGE with flashlight 15-30sec before EACH cast!
lures.tip: CHARGE THE GLOW or its useless. Dawn/dusk/murky ONLY.

lures.name: Blue Fox Vibrax 8g
lures.retrieve: JUST REEL: Steady retrieve. Count down for depth. Slow = barely feel blade.
lures.tip: ALWAYS use ball bearing swivel. Smaller for streams/shallow.

lures.name: Blue Fox Vibrax 10g
lures.retrieve: JUST REEL: Steady retrieve, bump bottom. Count down for depth.
lures.tip: Best multi-species search bait for unknown water.

lures.name: Keitech Swing Impact FAT 4.8"
lures.hook_weight: 5/0 weighted hook 1/8-1/4oz (or 4/0 Eagle Claw 1/4oz). Centering pin. Open water: 4/0-5/0 jighead 1/8-1/4oz.
lures.retrieve: JUST REEL: SLOW steady retrieve. Feel tail thump. Pause to flutter.
lures.tip: TwistLOCK/screw hook mandatory (tears). Super-glue head. Jighead=open water, weighted hook=cover.

lures.name: Keitech Swing Impact FAT 3.8"
lures.hook_weight: Gamakatsu SPR-Lock 3/0 1/8oz (PERFECT match). Open water: 3/0 jighead 1/8oz.
lures.retrieve: JUST REEL: Slow steady. Also excellent swim jig/chatterbait trailer.
lures.tip: SPR-Lock 3/0 1/8oz = confirmed ideal. Add centering pin. Jighead for open water.

lures.name: Keitech Swing Impact FAT 2.8"
lures.hook_weight: Eagle Claw ball head jig 1/16oz. Open water: 1/0 jighead. Cover: 1/0 EWG + 2g bullet TX.
lures.retrieve: JUST REEL: Slow-medium steady on spinning. Also: drop shot nose-hooked.
lures.tip: Jighead for open. TX rig (1/0 EWG + 2g bullet) for weedy cover. Spinning only.

lures.name: Fish Arrow Flash-J Shad 4"
lures.hook_weight: 1/0-2/0 jighead 1/8oz (open). Or 2/0 EWG + 3.5g bullet TX (cover). Or drop shot.
lures.retrieve: ROD MOVES: Slow steady (foil does the work). Twitch-and-glide on drop shot.
lures.tip: Internal ALUMINUM FOIL = unique flash. Jighead maximizes flash. TX rig for weeds.

lures.name: Lunker City Swimfish 3.75"
lures.retrieve: JUST REEL: Slow steady. Subtler action than Keitech. More durable.
lures.tip: More durable + subtler than Keitech. Budget-friendly. Weighted hook=cover, jighead=open.

lures.name: Gambler Little EZ 3.25"
lures.hook_weight: Eagle Claw ball head jig 1/16oz. Or 1/0 EWG + 2g bullet TX (cover).
lures.retrieve: JUST REEL: Slow steady. Twitch-and-swim (reel 2-3 turns, pause, repeat).
lures.tip: Extremely soft plastic = wide tail kick. Cold/clear water specialist.

lures.name: PowerBait Pogy Swim Shad 2"
lures.retrieve: JUST REEL: Slow retrieve. Under bobber 2-4ft for crappie. Vertical jig.
lures.tip: PANFISH bait (crappie, bluegill). Grab-and-go. PowerBait scent.

lures.name: Zoom Super Fluke 5.25" White Pearl
lures.retrieve: ROD MOVES: Twitch-twitch-PAUSE on SLACK LINE. Rod low. Bites on pause.
lures.setup: Expride Spinning (primary) or Aspro + DC (also works — both have leader).
lures.tip: SLACK LINE mandatory for dart. White = stained/muddy/night/shad. Sweep-set on bite.

lures.name: Zoom Super Fluke 5.25" WM Red
lures.retrieve: ROD MOVES: Same cadence. WM Red = clear water, sunny, near weeds.
lures.setup: Expride Spinning (primary) or Aspro + DC (also works — both have leader).
lures.tip: WM Red when you see bait 2ft+ below. White when you cant.

lures.name: Zoom Tiny Fluke 3" Smokin Shad
lures.hook_weight: DROP SHOT: #1 hook + 7-10g drop weight 12-24in below. Or 1/0 EWG weightless.
lures.retrieve: ROD MOVES: Drop shot: shake-shake-pause. Weightless: mini twitch-pause.
lures.tip: Drop shot is PRIMARY (per Zoom pros). When 5in fluke too big.

lures.name: Reaction Innovations Sweet Beaver 4.2"
lures.hook_weight: 4/0 EWG (cast) or 5/0 straight shank (flip). 7g bullet cast / 10g flip.
lures.retrieve: ROD MOVES: Let FALL (gliding spiral). Hop, drag, shake. Most bites on fall.
lures.tip: THE legendary beaver. Gliding spiral fall is unique. Peg 10g in cover, free-slide 7g open.

lures.name: Strike King Rage Space Monkey 4"
lures.retrieve: ROD MOVES: Hop 6-8in (4 appendages flare wildly). Shake. Slow swim.
lures.tip: 4 appendages = max displacement. Stained-dirty water (Black Blue). Spirals on fall.

lures.name: Strike King Rage Craw 4" (GP)
lures.hook_weight: 3/0 EWG. 7g bullet cast / 10g flip. Also: jig trailer (trim nose).
lures.retrieve: ROD MOVES: DRAG slowly (crawfish scoot). Hop 4-6in. Shake near beds.
lures.setup: Zodias MH + Revo (flip) or Aspro + DC (cast)
lures.tip: Big claws flap with minimal input. GP = universal. Also great jig trailer (Rage Craw behind pitching jig).

lures.name: Strike King Rage Craw 4" (WM Red)
lures.retrieve: ROD MOVES: Same. WM Red = clear + sunny + sandy/gravel bottom.
lures.tip: WM Red when clear+sun. GP when stained or unsure.

lures.name: Strike King Rage Bug 4"
lures.retrieve: ROD MOVES: Hop, drag, swim. More appendages = more vibration than Rage Craw.
lures.tip: Bulkier than Rage Craw = BEST JIG TRAILER in your box. Or standalone TX.

lures.name: Googan Baits Bandito Bug 4"
lures.retrieve: ROD MOVES: Drag, shake, hop. Slaunch Sauce scent.
lures.setup: Zodias MH + Revo or Aspro + DC (10.5g casts well)
lures.tip: Compact body with raised flanges. Slaunch Sauce scent. 10.5g casts well on baitcaster.

lures.name: Gary Yamamoto Zako 4"
lures.hook_weight: 3/0-4/0 EWG. 5-7g bullet. Also: weightless or C-rig.
lures.retrieve: ROD MOVES: Let fall (heavy salt = fast shimmy-fall). Drag, shake.
lures.setup: Zodias MH + Revo or Aspro + DC
lures.tip: NOT standard creature. Panfish SHAPE. Heavy Yamamoto salt = fast enticing fall. Clear water.

lures.name: Gary Yamamoto Senko 5" (GP)
lures.hook_weight: WACKY: #1 G-Lock + wacky ring. TX: 2/0 EWG + 5g bullet.
lures.retrieve: LET FALL: WACKY: Cast, let sink, DO NOT RETRIEVE. Watch line. 70% bites on fall.
lures.tip: THE single best bass lure. Dont retrieve. Wacky ring saves plastic. GP = any clarity.

lures.name: Gary Yamamoto Senko 5" (Blk/Blue)
lures.retrieve: LET FALL: Same. This color = stained/muddy/night/low light.
lures.tip: When GP disappears in murky water. Killer at night and in rain.

lures.name: Zoom Thick Worm (White)
lures.hook_weight: 4/0-5/0 EWG. TX: 5-10g bullet. C-rig: 14g egg + 18in leader.
lures.retrieve: ROD MOVES: TX: fall, drag, shake. C-rig: long sweeps. SWIM: slow steady 1-3ft off bottom.
lures.tip: WHITE WORM AT NIGHT = legendary. Slow crawl shallow banks after dark. Also: swim like a shad.

lures.name: Z-Man Finesse TRD (Ned Rig)
lures.retrieve: LET FALL: Cast, sink, drag 2-3in hops. LONG pauses. Dead simple.
lures.tip: Tail stands UP on bottom. Dont overthink. Less = more. Weedless for your weedy lakes.

lures.name: Coffee Tubes 3.5" Pro Model (GP)
lures.hook_weight: Internal tube jig 1/8oz = SPIRAL FALL (buy if you dont have). Or TX: 3/0 EWG + 3.5g bullet (weedless but no spiral).
lures.retrieve: ROD MOVES: Hop and drop on SLACK (allows spiral). Drag on rock. Dead-stick in cold.
lures.tip: Internal jig = signature CORKSCREW fall (nothing else does this). TX = weedless but loses spiral. Buy internal tube jigheads.

lures.name: Coffee Tubes 2.75" Fat Tube
lures.hook_weight: Internal tube jig 1/16oz (or 1/0 EWG + 2g bullet TX).
lures.retrieve: ROD MOVES: Same techniques, slower, more delicate. Lighter jig = slower spiral.
lures.tip: Spinning only. Multi-species (smallmouth, crappie, walleye). Ultra-clear/pressured.

lures.name: Yum 2" Grub (White)
lures.hook_weight: Eagle Claw ball head jig 1/16oz (or #4-1/0 jighead).
lures.retrieve: JUST REEL: Slow steady (curly tail thumps). Vertical jig for crappie over brush.
lures.tip: Most versatile multi-species lure ever. Catches everything. White = shad match.

lures.name: PowerBait 2" Power Grub (Pearl Wht)
lures.retrieve: JUST REEL: Same. PowerBait scent = 18x longer hold time (panfish advantage).
lures.tip: Better scent than Yum. Slightly softer = better action. Pearl White = universal.

lures.name: Dirty Jigs Pitchin' 3/8oz
lures.hook_weight: Mustad 5/0 (stock). ADD TRAILER: Rage Craw or Rage Bug.
lures.retrieve: ROD MOVES: PITCH to cover, watch line FALL. Hop 4-6in. Drag. Shake. 2-3 hops then move.
lures.tip: YOUR PRIMARY shallow jig. Best trailer: Rage Craw (crawfish) or Rage Bug (bulk). Peg in cover.

lures.name: Dirty Jigs Pitchin' 1/2oz
lures.hook_weight: Mustad 5/0 (stock). ADD TRAILER: Rage Craw or Rage Bug.
lures.retrieve: ROD MOVES: Same as 3/8. Use for: deeper (6-8ft), windy, punching weeds.
lures.tip: Step up from 3/8oz for depth/wind/thicker cover.

lures.name: Dirty Jigs 18.8g (various colors)
lures.hook_weight: 5/0 (stock). ADD TRAILER: Rage Craw or Rage Bug.
lures.retrieve: ROD MOVES: Same pitch-fall-hop technique. Heaviest = fast fall reaction.
lures.tip: Heavy duty for depth + thick cover. GP=clear, PBJ=stained, Blk/Blue=muddy.

lures.name: Swim Jig + Space Monkey (bladed)
lures.hook_weight: Stock 5/0 + blade + Space Monkey paddle trailer.
lures.retrieve: JUST REEL: STEADY swim ~1 turn/sec. Rod 9-10 oclock. Pause 2-3sec occasionally.
lures.setup: Zodias MH + Revo SX. 30-40lb braid + leader.
lures.tip: BAITFISH imitation (steady thump). Blade = stained water/wind. Remove blade if weeds foul.

lures.name: Swim Jig + Rage Craw (no blade)
lures.retrieve: JUST REEL: Steady swim with MORE PAUSES (claws flutter on fall). Along weed edges.
lures.setup: Zodias MH + Revo SX. 15-17lb fluoro or 30lb braid.
lures.tip: CRAWFISH imitation. Pauses key (claws flutter down). More versatile in weeds (no blade to foul).

lures.name: Spinnerbait 10g Col/Wil Silver
lures.retrieve: JUST REEL: Slow roll: BARELY feel Colorado thump. Medium for flash.
lures.setup: Aspro + DC (10g is light - DC handles it. Too light for Revo SX.)
lures.tip: YOUR #1 SPINNERBAIT from shore. 3/8oz = correct weight for 3-8ft. Most versatile config.

lures.name: Spinnerbait 17.6g Dbl Wil G/S
lures.retrieve: JUST REEL: Medium-fast (willows need speed to spin). Flash over thump.
lures.setup: Zodias MH + Revo SX or Aspro + DC
lures.tip: Good #2. Chartreuse for stained. Faster than Col/Wil. Wind + shad days.

lures.name: Spinnerbait 23.5g Dbl Col White
lures.retrieve: JUST REEL: ULTRA SLOW ROLL. Maximum vibration, zero flash. Cant go too slow.
lures.tip: MUDDY WATER / NIGHT ONLY. Double Colorado = feel-not-see. Slow as possible.

lures.name: Spinnerbait 18.5g Col/Wil G/S
lures.retrieve: JUST REEL: Slow roll to medium. Same versatile Col/Wil combo as your 10g but heavier = deeper + farther.
lures.setup: Zodias MH + Revo SX or Aspro + DC
lures.tip: Heavier version of your #1. Use when 10g cant reach fish (depth, wind, distance). Gold/silver blades slightly more flash than all-silver.

lures.name: Spinnerbait 22g Dbl Wil White
lures.retrieve: JUST REEL: Medium-fast to burn. White painted blades = subtle flash (not metallic glare).
lures.tip: White blades = more subtle than metallic. Good on bright sunny days when metallic flash spooks fish. Heavy = distance + deep.

lures.name: Spinnerbait 22g Dbl Wil Silver
lures.retrieve: JUST REEL: Fast burn or deep slow roll. Maximum shad imitation (all silver everything).
lures.tip: Pure shad school imitator. Burn near surface when bass busting shad. Also: pike bait (big flash profile).

lures.name: Spinnerbait 24.2g Dbl Wil G/S
lures.retrieve: JUST REEL: Fast burn surface or deep slow roll along bottom.
lures.tip: Very heavy. Deep water, extreme wind, or pike hunting. Not for typical 3-5ft shore fishing.

lures.name: Spinnerbait 24g Dbl Wil Gold
lures.retrieve: JUST REEL: Medium-fast. Gold blades = warmer flash tone, better in stained than silver.
lures.tip: Gold blades for stained/overcast. Chartreuse skirt adds visibility. Heavy = distance/deep/pike.

lures.name: Spinnerbait 27g Dbl Wil Red
lures.retrieve: JUST REEL: Fast burn (reaction) or slow deep roll. Most unusual color = change-up bait.
lures.tip: HEAVIEST. Red/black = crawfish or bluegill, NOT shad. Red triggers PIKE aggression. Use when white/silver gets refused. Reaction/change-up bait.
